title: Gas Exchange at the Alveoli
text:
Gas Exchange at the Alveoli
Anatomy & Physiology

Today I will explain how gas exchange occurs in the lungs, specifically at the alveoli.

title: Gas Exchange at the Alveoli
text:
What Are Alveoli?


Microscopic air sacs in the lungs




Located at the ends of bronchioles




Primary site of gas exchange



:::

Alveoli are tiny air sacs found at the ends of bronchioles. They are the main location where oxygen and carbon dioxide are exchanged between air and blood.

title: Gas Exchange at the Alveoli
text:
Structure Supports Function


Walls are one cell thick




Surrounded by capillaries




Large surface area



The structure of alveoli makes gas exchange efficient. Thin walls reduce diffusion distance, and capillaries bring blood very close to the air.

title: Gas Exchange at the Alveoli
text:
How Gas Exchange Occurs



Oxygen diffuses into the blood




Carbon dioxide diffuses into the alveoli




Movement occurs by diffusion



Oxygen moves from the alveoli into the blood because its concentration is higher in the alveoli. Carbon dioxide moves from the blood into the alveoli. This process happens by diffusion.

title: Gas Exchange at the Alveoli
text:
Why Alveoli Are Efficient


Short diffusion distance




Moist surface helps gases dissolve




Constant blood flow maintains gradients



These features allow gas exchange to happen quickly and continuously. Blood flow maintains concentration gradients so diffusion can keep occurring.

title: Gas Exchange at the Alveoli
text:
Summary


Alveoli are the functional units of the lungs




Specialized anatomy supports diffusion




Gas exchange is essential for cellular respiration



In summary, alveoli are specialized structures that allow oxygen to enter the bloodstream and carbon dioxide to be removed. This process is essential for cellular respiration and life.
